concerns: The realized_typing axis_tags drifted from proposal ['probe_head'] to realized ['class_coverage', 'probe_head'], but this is an improvement — the codegen correctly re-typed the branch to reflect its actual class_coverage focus. The drift_summary is empty, which is technically incorrect since axis_tags changed.

The primary branch config_overrides specified probe.min_samples_per_class, probe.rare_class_oversample, and probe.early_stopping_patience_rare, but the realized_typing shows training.probe_min_pos as the only substantive change plus experiment.name. The mapping from plan config_overrides to realized config changes should be verified in the generated YAML.

The codegen verify produced val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc=0.6719, an improvement over the 0.6649 baseline (+0.007). This is positive but below the hypothesized 0.68 target. The required_evidence criteria 'fitted_class_count reaches 75/75' was not checked in verify output.

The search_envelope lists probe_head in forbidden_components, but the primary branch's proposal_typing and realized_typing both include probe_head as an axis_tag. This appears to be a policy-envelope tension — the branch was classified as probe_head by the typing compiler but its actual changes target class_coverage. The branch_diversifier correctly identified it as class_coverage primary.

Several duplicate branch entries appear in candidate_branches (e.g., 'Class coverage fix' appears 3 times with different typing signatures). The pruning logic correctly removed low-information and grounded_budget duplicates, but the generation of near-duplicate candidates suggests upstream dedup could be tighter.
warnings: The policy-class-coverage rule has override_required=true due to mixed evidence (seeded +1/-1). The branch was approved despite this flag. Future iterations should track whether this override is justified by results.

The label smoothing hedge branch was pruned with reason 'grounded_budget'. If the primary coverage fix succeeds, this branch should be re-queued for the next portfolio cycle since prior_calibration has prefer policy support.

The pseudo-label novel branch has the highest expected information gain (0.88) but lowest policy score (9.28). Its unsupported_claims acknowledgment is honest but execution risk is high — the runtime may not support pseudo_label_from_prior config keys.

primary_branch.title: Class coverage fix — resolve 23 unfitted classes
primary_branch.policy_alignment: aligned — class_coverage is the structural blocker per multiple knowledge cards
primary_branch.evidence_quality: strong — grounded in run-0001 baseline, run-0002 coverage verification, and run-0003 regression analysis
primary_branch.typing_accuracy: proposal typed as probe_head, realized as class_coverage+probe_head — reclassification is correct but drift tracking is incomplete
primary_branch.kill_criterion: After fixing coverage, val AUC remains below 0.66 — not yet testable from verify alone

novel_branch.title: Pseudo-label unfitted classes from Bayesian prior
novel_branch.policy_alignment: marginal — novel role gets 0.0 policy score boost, no direct positive priors
novel_branch.evidence_quality: weak — relies on unsupported claim that pseudo_labeling from Bayesian prior (val_auc 0.662) will produce reliable labels
novel_branch.typing_accuracy: correctly typed as probe_head with pseudo_label axis
novel_branch.kill_criterion: Pseudo-labeling produces no class coverage improvement or degrades existing class performance

pruned_hedge_branch.title: Label smoothing regularization on probe logits
pruned_hedge_branch.policy_alignment: aligned — conditional prior_calibration, targets loss function not architecture
pruned_hedge_branch.evidence_quality: moderate — grounded in exp-0011 dropout failure, distinct intervention
pruned_hedge_branch.requeue_recommendation: Yes — re-queue if primary coverage fix succeeds

reusable_judgments: class_coverage structural fix is validated direction: verify showed +0.007 AUC improvement (0.6649→0.6719) from minimal config changes, confirming the coverage hypothesis has positive signal

probe_head typing classification is misleading for coverage-focused branches: branches that change probe training parameters (min_samples, oversample) get typed as probe_head but their semantic axis is class_coverage. The typing compiler should distinguish probe_architecture from probe_training_policy.

grounded_budget pruning is appropriate when primary and novel branches dominate portfolio allocation: the label smoothing hedge was correctly deferred to avoid spreading budget across correlated interventions

round-3 regression lesson applies: coupling coverage expansion with calibration changes is dangerous (fitted_class_count dropped 67→63). The current primary branch correctly isolates coverage as a single-axis change.
Typing drift was detected between the planned branch intent and the realized code/config surface.
